rationale: The cited 2000 paper is a core reference-price brand-choice model (IRP/ERP with latent segments in scanner data), and the citing 2009 paper explicitly positions itself as extending prior reference-price choice models beyond single-point formulations. Given this strong construct/method overlap (reference prices in packaged-goods brand choice) and plausible role as a foundational comparator, this is not an obvious incidental mention.
